review: THIS IS BY FAR THE WORST LOCATION I HAVE EVER BEEN TO!Never have I been so utterly disrespected and disregarded by so many associates in one location at one time. I visited a Walmart location a few days ago and used a self checkout kiosk. I mistakenly asked for cash back from the kiosk (which does not dispense cash) and left the store without realizing I never received my change.  I called in to customer service, and was advised over the phone by one customer service associate (Ronidqua) to bring in my receipt to receive the cash back from my transaction. I came into the store, and waited 45-minutes in line to get assistance. Once at the customer service counter, I explained my situation, to which the associate, Candice, takes my receipt and tells me to have a seat because my issue will take a while.  After 20-minutes of waiting, I follow up with Candice to make sure someone is handling my issue.  She says that she just followed up with someone about my issue and walks off to handle returned items. After ANOTHER 45-minutes of waiting patiently, I ask to speak to a manager (Rolando). After explaining my issue to him, he tells me that I should first contact my banking institution to handle this matter and if they can't do anything, then to come back to the store.  I ask him if it would be possible to at least get my original receipt back, to which he responds no member of his staff would have taken a receipt and given it to a member of security to rectify an issue.  He then proceeds to tell me that if my financial institution is unable to resolve the issue, then I should come back and they can re-print me a copy of my receipt.  He made ZERO attempt to recover my original receipt or even acknowledge the fact that one of his associates DID execute such an act.  After multiple requests to get my original receipt returned to me, I asked if he could get the associate that took my receipt from me. He responded that she had left for the evening.  The level of complete and utter lack of adequate customer service at this location is very disturbing. There have been several instances with similar outcomes where I leave highly frustrated at the lack of care associates take in fulfilling even their basic responsibilities. There is obviously a lack of training present, both in basic customer service and in problem solving. Instances like these make it increasingly daunting to patron these locations because of the EXPECTATION of poor customer service and lack of care by almost ALL employed associates.  This type of quality is not indicative of the Walmart mission or vision and needs to be addressed immediately.  No one should ever be subjected to the blatant disregard for people or their time that I experienced.This was the final straw for me spending my money with a company that could clearly care less about quality customer satisfaction or even basic customer service.  I will no longer shop here and fully intend to spread the word about the poor service I received today.

review: I was actually glad when this Walmart opened because of the dearth of businesses in so many south side neighborhoods.  Even though Walmart is a "big box" retailer with some not so attractive attributes I was looking forward to something that would be better than nothing.  Social consciousness, eco-friendly gobbledy gook, and feelgood slogans aside, what good is a business that won't open in my neighborhood?  Alas, this Walmart manages to be sub-par even with my generally low expectations of a "big box" retailer.  I've found pricing to be pretty good on most items.  I commonly find OTC pharmacy items are much less than competitors.   General merchandise is poorly stocked and yet the aisles are perpetually cluttered with boxes.  Things are messy, dirty, and misplaced.  The employees are a mixed bag.  I've had a few efficient and polite ones and some that are rude and unhelpful.  Some days you can get in and out, other days the lines are so long and slow moving that I've dropped the merchandise on the nearest shelf and walked out.  The thing that finally turned me off was going in one night to find a jewelry organizer.  After looking around for a few minutes in jewelry I couldn't find it so I approach the jewelry counter to ask if it might be located elsewhere.  Before I can open my mouth some armed security guard tells me very rudely to step back.  I eventually figured out that the sales girl was counting money to close the register but nothing in my dress or demeanor would indicate that I was there to steal.  Trying to ask where I might be able to find help got another rude brushoff.  I draw the line at being treated rudely and disrespectfully when I'm there to give a business my money.  This Walmart can hire armed guards to mean-mug customers but they can't hire anyone capable of answering a question?  No thanks! I think I've been back one time since that incident to get something very minor.  I have to be in pretty dire straits to deal with this place.  I'm sure they'll do fine without my money but I'd rather drive farther and be able to shop in peace.

review: I am not a fan of Wal-Mart in general because of their practices. Because of this, I have cut down my spending from about 90% to 20-30% in the last few years. This particular store has huge issues and can be a big disappointment with overall customer relations. You never know if you will get the services you need. For example, I ordered a same day photo book in 2013 as a gift. NEVER RECEIVED IT. Went to the store several times and there was always a different excuse.  Today I was there (only because I was a passenger with someone else who decided to stop in). Came across the clearance aisles and saw 1/4 of a wall stocked with Cherrios under huge "Clearance" signs. YES!! Well, they didn't have prices on them so I asked a co-manager, Patrick, for the price. He checks with another employee then informs me that they aren't on sale. Excuse me?! Then why are they on the wall marked clearance. Long story short, he argues that they are changing the wall (although NOT ACTIVELY) and that's it. Even threatened to have my mom thrown out the store if she took a picture. Most places will acknowledge the mistake, and give you a discount. But not Patrick, he was steadfast in making sure Wal-Mart didn't lose a dollar. This just confirms my lack of desire to shop there. Oh, and let's talk about how they have the generic white bags with red thank you on it. Talk about bad management! Does corporate know you are not using Wal-Mart brand bags? Wonder what they will think about that. I will say, however, that my cashier, Wakenda was very pleasant as well as the young man in electronics that assisted us with the IPods. Only reason I'm not complaining about being forced to give them one star.

review: I went to the Walmart location on 95th and Western earlier today to pick up some toiletries, but the lines were too long. I then commuted to the Walmart location at 8331 S. Stewart Ave. to get the same products. The Quilted Northern Bath Tissue 24-roll double roll pack  was priced at $11.97 at the Walmart location on 95th and Western, but when I attempted to purchase the same exact item at 8331 S. Stewart it  costs $13.47. This is a $1.50 difference in price. Why the difference? I feel that  prices are different because of the contrasting racial make-ups of each neighborhood. The Walmart on 95th and Western is within a neighborhood where white people are the majority.  The Walmart on 83rd St. is in a predominantly black neighborhood. I question if this is a blatant reference to how Walmart values African-American money as opposed to the money of white people. In the next few weeks I will conduct a study, comparing the prices of the same exact products at different Walmart locations in neighborhoods that have dissimilar racial compositions.

The cashier told the woman that her address on her check did not match the address on her license so she could not cash her check. The woman went to the cashier's supervisor and again was told no. Then the woman went to an assistant Manager. Meanwhile my friend and I were next. As we step up, the young said to her co-worker "Didn't nobody tell her to move." Implying that the lady should not have moved to a new address. Then the assistant manager showed up and told the cashier that he was going to override the check and cash the woman's check. The cashier said, "Why are you letting her cash her check instead of backing me up?" The assistant manager said he already signed off on it....Bottom line, the cashier was wrong and should not have said the things she said about that woman and when her manager took responsibility for cashing the check, it was not the cashier's place to question his judgement. review: I try my best not to go to this Walmart because it is always crowded with under stocked shelves. I have possibly visited a dozen times since it has opened as I like the Hodgkin and Villa Park Walmart's better. Many of the times when I come there is a problem with the "BAGGING OF GROCERIES".So I'm weird, I like my cold foods separate from my dry foods separate from my toiletries and such, GO FIGURE. I always ask kindly and say thank you repeatedly. Recently I went to this Walmart and asked the lady to put a frozen food item separate from a dry food item. She said okay but then I realized that she was bagging my frozen groceries with dry goods again. This could not be difficult as they have a 6 bag spinning thingy. It's not like she didn't have options. I brought it to her attention again, saying please and thank you (hoping kindness would help)  but it didn't work. So I just gave up and let her bag my $130 worth of food and household items how she wanted.Afterwards, I get to my car and the the push down top of my very large bottle of dish liquid was broken off and the dish liquid was spilling out everywhere.
